text.title: CI Eval Gate Config — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for a robust LLM eval gate in CI. Each addresses a specific failure mode that has bitten teams shipping LLM features.

rule.statement: The baseline JSON MUST live in the repo (e.g., `.evals/baseline.json`) and update ONLY via a dedicated PR with title pattern `chore(evals): rebase baseline ...`. Baseline updates from feature branches are rejected by CI itself.
rule.rationale: If the baseline can be updated alongside the feature change, the gate is a fig leaf — devs will smuggle regressions into the baseline. Separating the rebase PR makes the change reviewable and reversible.

rule.statement: Thresholds are configured per metric, NOT as a single pass/fail. Typical config: accuracy drop <= 2 percentage points OR refusal rate increase <= 1pp OR latency p95 increase <= 10% OR cost-per-eval increase <= 5%. The gate fails if ANY metric exceeds its threshold (AND logic, not OR).

rationale.source: OpenAI Evals + Promptfoo CI docs, internal LLMOps reports

rule.rationale: Single-metric gates miss regressions in untracked dimensions. A prompt change that boosts accuracy 1pp but doubles cost is a regression most teams want to catch. Per-metric thresholds force explicit decisions about what you trade.

rule.statement: When a case is confirmed flaky (passes ≥ 30% AND fails ≥ 30% across runs on unchanged code), it moves to a quarantine list with a re-evaluation date (max 30 days). Quarantined cases do not gate PRs but appear in a dashboard. After re-evaluation date, case must either return to active set or be retired with rationale.

rationale.source: Flaky-test research from Microsoft and Google CI teams, ML eval reproducibility studies

rule.rationale: Without quarantine, flaky cases either block PRs spuriously (devs stop trusting the gate) OR get silently disabled (gate erodes). Time-boxed quarantine forces resolution.

rule.statement: An override of a failing gate requires: explicit override-record file with named approver (NOT the PR author), rationale (sentence), expected revert plan (e.g., "follow-up PR within 7 days to address regression"). Approver MUST be on a documented override-approver list (tech lead, ML lead). Override is audited monthly.
rule.rationale: Without named approver + audit, overrides become routine. With them, overrides are deliberate exceptions. Audit is what prevents the override path from becoming the default path.

rule.statement: Cost-per-eval MUST be one of the gated metrics. A prompt or model change that increases cost per eval by > 5% without explicit override is blocked. Includes token cost AND tool-call cost (if agent eval).

rationale.source: LLM cost-surge case studies (multiple post-mortems on GPT-4 -> GPT-4-turbo migrations), unit-economics research

rule.rationale: Without a cost gate, prompt engineering quietly inflates per-request cost until unit economics break. Many teams discovered 3x cost increases only at month-end billing. Gating cost makes the trade explicit at PR time.

example.title: Per-metric thresholds + override record
example.code:
PR #1402: "Switch summary chain from gpt-4o to claude-3-5-haiku"
Baseline vs PR results: accuracy: 87.4% -> 87.1% (delta -0.3pp, within 2pp threshold) PASS refusal_rate: 2.1% -> 2.3% (delta +0.2pp, within 1pp threshold) PASS latency_p95: 3.2s -> 2.4s (delta -25%, within 10% threshold) PASS cost_per_eval: $0.012 -> $0.004 (delta -67%, within 5% threshold) PASS json_schema_valid: 99.2% -> 99.1% (delta -0.1pp, within 0.5pp threshold) PASS
Decision: PASS (all metrics within threshold)
No override needed. PR proceeds; baseline NOT updated by this PR. Separate "chore(evals): rebase baseline for haiku switch" PR follows to rebaseline so future PRs measure against the new haiku baseline.
example.note: Illustrates r1, r2, r5: per-metric thresholds, baseline isolation, cost included.

example.title: Single-metric gate + silent flaky
example.code:
PR #1403: "Improve prompt for question classifier"
Baseline vs PR: accuracy: 89% -> 91% -> single-metric gate passes
What was actually true: cost_per_eval: $0.008 -> $0.024 (3x; not gated) latency_p95: 1.8s -> 4.5s (2.5x; not gated) Three cases flipped between runs (flaky; silently removed by author "to make CI green").
Production impact 2 weeks later: $2.4k/month cost surge, p95 latency complaints from users.
Repair: enforce r2, r3, r5. Per-metric thresholds catch cost + latency. Flaky cases go to quarantine, not deletion.
example.note: Illustrates r2, r3, r5 violations — single-metric gates miss the slow leaks.
